title: Set Window Height — {key:z}{n}{key:Enter}
subtitle: Resize the current window to {n} lines, scroll cursor's line to the top.
summary: z{n} followed by Enter resizes the current window to n lines tall and lands the cursor at the first non-blank of the topmost line.

blocks.text: An obscure but occasionally perfect command: {key:z}{key:1}{key:0}{key:Enter} resizes the current window to 10 lines and scrolls the cursor's line to the top.

blocks.label: Resize window to {n} lines, cursor's line to top

blocks.title: Two Things at Once
blocks.text:
{key:z}{n}{key:Enter} does *two* operations atomically:

  1. Sets `'winheight'` for the current window to `{n}` lines (same effect as `:resize` {n}).
  2. Scrolls so the cursor's current line becomes the top of the window (same as {key:z}{key:t}).

Useful pattern: working in a file with multiple splits, you want one specific window to be exactly N lines tall to fit some block. Move cursor to the start of that block, {key:z}{key:N}{key:Enter}, done.

Without the count, {key:z}{key:Enter} is just "scroll cursor's line to top *and* go to first non-blank" — same as {key:z}{key:+}'s landing variant.

rows: Resize to {n} lines + scroll line to top
Cursor line to top + first non-blank
